---
canvas:
  allowed_extensions:
  - pdf
  grading_type: pass_fail
  group_assignment: true
  group_set: Project Groups
  points: 1
  published: false
  submission_types:
  - online_upload
  type: assignment
title: Lab 7 – P-Channel MOSFET with Level Shifter
---

## Learning Goals

- Understand high-side switching with a P-channel MOSFET
- Use an N-channel MOSFET (2N7000) as a logic level shifter
- Learn how motor driver ICs combine N-channel and P-channel MOSFETs internally

## Background

A **P-channel MOSFET** turns ON when $V_{GS}$ is **negative** — i.e., the gate is pulled **below** the source. For a P-channel MOSFET with its source connected to 12V, we need to pull the gate to GND to turn it on.

Problem: the ESP32 outputs 0–3.3V, but the P-channel gate needs to swing between GND and 12V. Solution: use a **2N7000** (small N-channel MOSFET) as a level shifter.

**How it works:**

- ESP32 GPIO = HIGH (3.3V) → 2N7000 turns ON → pulls IRF9540 gate to GND → IRF9540 turns ON → load is powered
- ESP32 GPIO = LOW (0V) → 2N7000 turns OFF → 10kΩ pull-up pulls IRF9540 gate to 12V → IRF9540 turns OFF

This is exactly the circuit used in the H-bridge (Lab 8–9) for the high-side switches.

## Components

- ESP32 DevKit
- 1× IRF9540 (P-channel MOSFET)
- 1× 2N7000 (N-channel MOSFET, level shifter)
- 1× LED + resistor for 12V
- 1× 10kΩ pull-up resistor (IRF9540 gate to 12V)
- 1× 10kΩ pull-down resistor (2N7000 gate to GND)
- 12V power supply

## Tasks

1. Build the level-shifter circuit:
   - **IRF9540:** Source to 12V, Drain to LED+resistor to GND, Gate to 2N7000 drain
   - **2N7000:** Source to GND, Drain to IRF9540 gate (with 10kΩ pull-up to 12V), Gate to ESP32 GPIO (with 10kΩ pull-down to GND)
2. Test with blink code. Verify the 12V LED is controlled from the ESP32.
3. **Measure** the IRF9540 gate voltage when the LED is ON and when OFF.
4. Apply PWM to dim the LED. Does it work?

## Questions

1. Draw the complete circuit including both MOSFETs, both resistors, the LED, and the ESP32 GPIO. Label all voltages.
2. What happens if you connect the ESP32 GPIO directly to the IRF9540 gate (without the 2N7000)? Why doesn't it work?
3. An H-bridge uses P-channel (high-side) and N-channel (low-side) MOSFETs. Can you see how this lab connects to the next one?

## Submission

Write a short lab report in Quarto following the [Report Writing Guide](../01_Fundamentals/01_Report_Writing_Guide.qmd). Include a hand-drawn circuit diagram with voltage annotations, your gate voltage measurements, and answers to the questions. Render to PDF and upload.
